offline.name: Lok Sabha (LS.75)
offline.agenda: Legislative, Judicial, and Political Reckoning of the Internal Emergency (1975–77): Accountability, Constitutional Restoration, and the Stability of Post-Emergency Governance.
offline.description:
June 24, 1975.
A court ruling shakes the Prime Minister’s authority. Political legitimacy stands questioned. Within hours, whispers of extraordinary measures grow louder.

Should executive power be defended at all costs? Or must constitutional morality prevail over political survival?

As Parliament stands on the brink of an unprecedented decision, this committee will confront the fragile balance between authority, judicial oversight, and the temptation of emergency powers.

History will not remember hesitation, only the choices made.

offline.name: All India Political Parties Meet (AIPPM)
offline.agenda: Deliberation on the Electoral Bonds Scheme and the Use of Central Investigative Agencies in Political Competition: Transparency, Coercion, and Democratic Fairness.
offline.description:
Democracy thrives on competition.
But what happens when the rules of competition are contested?

Opaque funding channels. Selective investigations. Allegations of coercion. Questions of transparency.

Are electoral bonds a reform or a veil? Are investigative agencies instruments of accountability? Or are these tools of leverage?

In this chamber, parties must negotiate not just policy, but trust itself.

offline.name: United Nations Security Council (UNSC)
offline.agenda: Responding to the Expansion of Armed Extremist Activity in the Sahel Region: State Collapse, Foreign Military Presence, and Civilian Protection.
offline.description:
Borders blur. Militias expand. Foreign powers intervene. Civilians flee.

Across the Sahel region, fragile states struggle under the weight of insurgency, climate stress, and geopolitical rivalry. As extremist networks consolidate power, international actors debate intervention, sovereignty, and responsibility.

Is military presence stabilising, or escalating?
Can humanitarian protection coexist with strategic interests?

The Security Council must decide whether it can contain collapse before it spreads.

offline.name: Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC)
offline.agenda: Regulation of Politically Polarising Cinema in India: Certification Disputes and State Responses to The Kerala Story, The Kashmir Files, Article 370, and 72 Hoorain.
offline.description:
A film releases. Protests erupt. Courts intervene. States respond.

Cinema has become more than storytelling; it is memory, identity, and politics projected on a national screen.
Should the state regulate narratives that inflame public sentiment? Or must artistic freedom remain inviolable, even when uncomfortable?

This committee sits at the uneasy intersection of free expression and public order, where every certification decision carries political weight.

offline.name: United Nations General Assembly (UNGA)
offline.agenda: Failure of Multilateral Institutions in Preventing Mass Civilian Casualties in Urban Warfare; Deliberation on the Use of Economic Sanctions as Instruments of Warfare, with Reference to Sanctions on Russia, Iran, and Venezuela and Their Humanitarian Impact.
offline.description:
Sanctions promise accountability without bullets.
But they rarely remain bloodless.

As nations deploy economic restrictions against Russia, Iran, and Venezuela, global markets shift and civilian populations absorb the shock.

Are sanctions legitimate tools of pressure; or collective punishment under another name?

This Assembly must confront the ethical and humanitarian cost of economic warfare.

offline.name: Indian Premier League (IPL)
offline.description:
Beyond sport lies strategy.

Franchises balance budgets, public expectations, and tactical depth. Every bid alters the season before it begins. Alliances form, rivalries intensify, and calculated risks define champions.

In this high-stakes simulation, decision-making is immediate and unforgiving, where economics meets ambition on the auction floor.

offline.name: International Press (IP)
offline.description:
Power operates in rooms.
Perception operates everywhere else.

The International Press observes, investigates, and narrates the proceedings across committees. Through reporting, interviews, and editorial judgement, it shapes public understanding of institutional choices.

In politics, influence does not belong only to decision-makers, it belongs to those who tell the story.

online.name: United Nations Commission on the Status of Women (UNCSW)
online.agenda: Deliberation on Sexual Violence as a Weapon of War, with Reference to Investigations in Ukraine and Sudan.
online.description:
In conflict zones, violence extends beyond battlefields.

From Ukraine to Sudan, allegations of systematic sexual violence raise urgent questions of accountability and enforcement. When bodies become battlegrounds, international law is tested in its most fragile form.

Can justice be pursued amidst active conflict?
And who ensures enforcement when states fail?

This committee confronts one of war’s most devastating realities.

online.name: Indo-Pakistan Bilateral Talks (IPBT)
online.agenda: Lock Date: 27 February 2019 — Following cross-border air operations, aerial engagements, and the capture of an Indian Air Force pilot.
online.description:
Lock Date: 27 February 2019

Aerial engagements. Captured pilots. Escalating rhetoric.

Following cross-border operations and diplomatic strain, two nuclear-armed neighbours stand at a precarious threshold.
Will dialogue de-escalate tension, or harden positions further?

In this bilateral simulation, restraint may be as consequential as retaliation.

online.name: Historic United Nations Security Council (HUNSC)
online.agenda: 1971 Bangladesh War — Operation Searchlight, Refugee Crisis, Mukti Bahini Insurgency, Indo-Soviet Treaty, Escalation, Pre-emptive Airstrikes, Operation Poseidon, and the Surrender in Dhaka.
online.description:
Humanitarian catastrophe unfolds. Sovereignty is contested. Superpowers maneuver.

As the Bangladesh Liberation War intensifies, the Security Council grapples with intervention, recognition, and the limits of international law.

In a continuous crisis setting, decisions will evolve in real time, as diplomacy struggles to keep pace with war.

online.name: Lok Sabha
online.agenda: Scrutiny of Executive Exemptions and State Surveillance Powers Under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
online.description:
Data defines the modern state.

The Act promises protection, yet grants executive exemptions in the name of security and governance. Where does privacy end and surveillance begin?

Parliament must determine how far state authority should extend into digital lives.

This is not a debate about technology. It is a debate about power.

online.name: BRICS Summit
online.description:
Currency is more than commerce, it is influence.

As BRICS nations consider reducing reliance on the U.S. dollar, questions of financial sovereignty, systemic reform, and global stability emerge.

Is de-dollarisation a strategic necessity, or a destabilising gamble?

This summit will test the future architecture of global finance.

online.name: Secret Committee
online.description:
Information is limited.
Clarity is scarce.
Decisions will be made with incomplete knowledge.

Participants enter without certainty, and must navigate ambiguity, intelligence leaks, and shifting realities.

The less you know, the more it matters how you think.
